nameMeanings.name: Farley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Farley, of which there are examples in Berkshire, Derbyshire, Hampshire, Kent, Somerset, Gloucestershire, Staffordshire, Surrey, Wiltshire, Shropshire, and Sussex. From Old English fearn ‘fern’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. See also Farleigh , Fairley , Fairlie .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Fearghaile (see Farrelly ).

variants.name: Farleigh
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Farleigh, of which there are examples in Hampshire, Devon, Kent, Somerset, Surrey, and Wiltshire. From Old English fearn ‘fern’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’. See also Farley , Fairley , Fairlie .

variants.name: Farlow
meanings: English: habitational name from a place in Shropshire, so named from Old English fearn ‘fern’ + hlāw ‘hill, tumulus’. This surname may have become confused with Farley .

variants.name: Fearnley
meanings: English (Yorkshire): from any of various places called with Old English fearn ‘fern, bracken’ + lēah ‘wood, clearing’, notably the Yorkshire places Farnley near Otley, Farnley near Armley, Farnley Tyas, Fearnley in Wyke, Fernley Hill in Altofts, and perhaps also Fernilee in Derbyshire.

variants.name: Farlee
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Farley  .

nameMeanings.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

nameMeanings.name: Alfred
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English name derived from ælf ‘elf, supernatural being’ + ræd ‘counsel’. It was a relatively common name before the Norman Conquest of Britain, being borne most notably by Alfred the Great ( 849–899 ), King of Wessex. After the Conquest it was adopted by the Normans in a variety of more or less radically altered forms ( see Avery ). In some regions the forms Alvery and Avery never fell entirely out of favour and became locally popular in the 16th century. It provides a rare example ( Edward is another) of a distinctively Old English name that has spread widely on the Continent. It was strongly revived in the 19th century, along with other names of pre-Conquest historical figures, faded in the mid-20th century, but has since recovered some popularity.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

en.lifeSummary: John Alfred Farley was born on 29 July 1506, in Great Bromley, Essex, England as the son of George Farley. He married Agnes Morthdon in 1530, in Great Bromley, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died on 23 August 1581, in Great Bromley, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 75.

Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé le 23 août 1581, à Great Bromley, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 75 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: John Alfred Farleyは1506年7月29日にイギリスエセックスグレアト ブロムリーでGeorge Farleyの息子として生まれた。1530年にイギリスエセックスグレアト ブロムリーでAgnes Morthdonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼は1581年8月23日にイギリスイギリスエセックスグレアト ブロムリーで75歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John Alfred Farley(은)는 1506년 7월 29일 영국 에세스 그리트 브롬리에서 George Farley의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1530년 영국 에세스 그리트 브롬리에서 Agnes Morthdon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1581년 8월 23일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 그리트 브롬리에서 75세의 나이로 사망했다.
